title: Nuclear Physics and Radioactivity
description: Atomic structure and nuclide notation, the four types of radioactive decay and nuclear equations, the random nature of decay, the exponential decay law and half-life, and mass-energy equivalence, binding energy, fission and fusion.
objectives: Interpret nuclide notation (mass number A, proton number Z) and identify isotopes
Write and balance nuclear equations for alpha, beta-minus, beta-plus and gamma decay
Compare the penetrating power and ionising ability of alpha, beta and gamma radiation
Explain why radioactive decay is random and spontaneous, and relate activity to the decay constant via A = λN
Derive and apply the exponential decay law N = N₀e^(-λt) and the half-life relation T½ = ln2/λ
Use mass-energy equivalence (E = mc²) to calculate mass defect and binding energy, and relate the binding-energy-per-nucleon curve to fission and fusion

lessons.title: Nuclear structure and types of decay
Activity, half-life and the decay law
Mass-energy equivalence, binding energy, fission and fusion
